And the second question is, where will they stay? So CNN has learned that they were going to stay at both a private residence and a royal residence, the second of which they would automatically receive police protection.
paragraphs.cn: 他们会在伦敦以外的地方与他同行吗？我们知道他将在英国各地参加活动，其中一站是第二大城市伯明翰，为明年的"不可征服运动会"预热。第二个问题是，他们会住在哪里？CNN了解到，他们原本计划住在一处私人住宅和一处王室住所，后者会自动获得警察保护。

paragraphs.speaker: Economics Reporter
paragraphs.text: But now that's all thrown into doubt, and there was further confusion sowed when Buckingham Palace clarified that an invitation by King Charles to stay at a royal residence has not been accepted by Prince Harry. So big question marks over this. But we'll find out not -- in the not too distant future.
paragraphs.cn: 但现在这一切都变得不确定了。白金汉宫澄清说，查尔斯国王邀请哈里王子入住王室住所的提议尚未被接受，这进一步增加了困惑。因此，这存在重大疑问。不过，我们将在不久的将来得知结果。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Yes, indeed. I'm sure they will figure out some type of resolution. They have to, right? Anna Cooban, thank you so much. Well, next up, a high stakes meeting in the nation's capital. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu could be visiting President Trump. So what is that going to look like? Stay with us.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，确实如此。我相信他们会找到某种解决方案。他们必须这样做，对吧？安娜·库班，非常感谢。接下来，在首都华盛顿将举行一场高风险会议。以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡可能会访问特朗普总统。那会是什么样子？请继续关注。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: CNN has learned that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is expected to visit the White House as soon as next Monday after the NATO summit ahead of Netanyahu's White House visit, Trump telling Axios that Netanyahu, quote, "knows who the boss is." The two leaders have not met since the end of February when the joint U.S.-Israeli war on Iran began. CNN has contacted the White House for a comment.
paragraphs.cn: CNN获悉，以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡预计最早将于下周一访问白宫，时间在北大西洋公约组织峰会之后。在内塔尼亚胡访问白宫之前，特朗普告诉Axios，内塔尼亚胡"知道谁是老板"。两位领导人自2月底美以联合对伊朗开战以来未曾会面。CNN已联系白宫寻求评论。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The NATO summit takes place this week in Turkey, and President Trump will be attending, even though he has threatened to withdraw from the alliance. The President recently said that he would not have gone to the summit, but the President of the host country, Turkey, asked him to attend. Let's discuss with Jim Townsend. He is the former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for European and NATO policy. Thank you so much for joining us.
paragraphs.cn: 北约峰会本周在土耳其举行，特朗普总统将出席，尽管他曾威胁要退出该联盟。总统最近表示，他本不会去参加峰会，但东道国土耳其总统邀请他出席。让我们与吉姆·汤森德讨论。他曾担任负责欧洲和北约政策的国防部副助理部长。非常感谢您加入我们。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Since President Trump already said he really did not want to attend the NATO summit, but is doing it out of respect for Turkey's President, do you think that NATO members will be able to appease Trump?
paragraphs.cn: 既然特朗普总统已经说过他其实不想参加北约峰会，但出于对土耳其总统的尊重才去，您认为北约成员国能否安抚特朗普？

paragraphs.speaker: Jim Townsend
paragraphs.text: Well, they're going to make a great effort to do that. And NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte will as well. And he was in Washington last week making a big pitch, telling Americans as well as Donald Trump what NATO can do for the U.S. and does for the U.S., and how much the allies are putting into their own defense. So there's a good news story to tell if Trump is listening.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他们会为此做出巨大努力。北约秘书长马克·吕特也会如此。他上周在华盛顿大力宣传，告诉美国人和唐纳德·特朗普，北约能为美国做什么、为美国做了什么，以及盟国在自身防务上投入了多少。所以，如果特朗普愿意听，这是一个好消息。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: NATO did send a congratulatory tweet as well to the U.S., recognizing the 250th anniversary of our independence. Do you think this was formality? Do you think this was a sign of how leaders will approach Trump? And what do they want from the U.S. as the alliance has been increasingly strained under the Trump presidency?
paragraphs.cn: 北约还向美国发了一条祝贺推文，纪念美国独立250周年。您认为这是形式主义吗？您认为这是各国领导人将如何对待特朗普的信号吗？在特朗普执政期间，联盟关系日益紧张，他们想从美国得到什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Well, I think it was formality. This is something that NATO does for every country, every ally that has a national day. But on this particular anniversary for the U.S., I think they couldn't miss it for sure. And I think they made a special effort too. And I think that will be the tone, frankly, among a lot of the allies is congratulating Trump and also the Americans for the 250th anniversary.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为这是形式主义。北约对每个有国庆日的盟国都会这样做。但在这个特殊的美国周年纪念日，我认为他们肯定不能错过。而且我认为他们也做出了特别努力。坦率地说，我认为这将是许多盟国的基调，即祝贺特朗普和美国人迎来250周年。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: And, you know, some of the allies were sending aircraft to the U.S., some of their acrobatic aircraft that were making flyovers. The French did that and the Brits. So there's been a lot of activity to try to show the U.S. that there certainly is a care and love coming from Europe. And I think what they both want to see is, in terms of the United States, more of a European effort and that's being made. And from Europe, they want to see a U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 而且，你知道，一些盟国向美国派遣了飞机，一些特技飞机进行了飞越。法国和英国都这样做了。所以有很多活动试图向美国表明，欧洲确实有关心和爱。我认为他们双方都希望看到的是，就美国而言，欧洲做出更多努力，而这正在实现。从欧洲方面来说，他们希望看到一个真正承诺留在联盟内的美国，或许言辞也能缓和一些。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: that really commits to stay within the alliance and maybe a softening of the rhetoric.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普以不掩饰自己对任何事情的感受或情绪而闻名，尤其是在北约问题上。因此，进入这些会谈时，我们将观察不同政要和民选官员如何在这个世界舞台上讨论世界事务，甚至更多地考虑以色列对伊朗的打击，您认为他会继续公开抨击他们吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Trump not known to hide his feelings or emotions on pretty much anything, especially when it comes to NATO. So, going into these talks, as we're going to be watching how the different dignitaries and elected officials are discussing world matters on this world stage, thinking more about the Israeli strikes on Iran even, do you expect him to continue lashing out them publicly?
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道，这是一个非常重要的问题。在特朗普看来，即使马克·吕特等人尽了最大努力向他表明，他的关注点在欧洲得到了倾听，他仍然固守着关于搭便车和北约是纸老虎的论调。我认为我们只能 --  -- 你知道，这就是他的风格，像一张唱片。他固守这一点，并且会说出来。但只要他不采取行动，这就是关键。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Well, you know, that's a really important point. It seems with Trump, even after the best efforts of Mark Rutte and others to show Trump that his, you know, his attention has been getting -- is being heard in Europe. He still is stuck on these talking points about freeloading and NATO as a paper tiger. And I think we've got to just -- you know, this is what he is like a record. He's stuck on it and he's going to say it. But as long as he doesn't act on it, that's the key.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，许多欧洲国家正在试图对冲风险，以防他真说"看，我退出联盟了"。欧洲正在进行大量工作，以增强欧洲的常规威慑能力。因此，他们确保在倾听特朗普言论的同时，也采取行动表明他们能够承担负担。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: And of course, a lot of European nations are trying to hedge their bets to be ready just in case he does say, look, I'm done with the alliance. There's a lot of work going on in Europe to build up the European capabilities for conventional deterrence. And so, they're making sure that they're listening to what Trump is saying and they're acting as well to show that they can carry the burden.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Do you see there being a material difference coming out of this NATO summit in the peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为这次北约峰会在美国与伊朗的和平谈判中会产生实质性差异吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Well, you know, it's interesting. I'm not sure we're going to see much coming out of the summit concerning the Middle East and those talks. Maybe NATO might decide to do a maritime support operation in the Gulf to help clear the mines. I hope they do something like that. But I think what we might see is something dealing with the Ukraine- Russia war.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道，这很有趣。我不确定峰会在中东和那些谈判方面会有多大成果。也许北约可能会决定在波斯湾开展海上支援行动，帮助清除水雷。我希望他们能这样做。但我认为我们可能会看到一些与乌克兰-俄罗斯战争有关的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Not only a strong support for Ukraine and potentially some money coming from allies to support Ukraine as well, but an emphasis from the United States that we're -- that the U.S. is going to be willing to head up talks again with Russia. Putin and Trump have already talked about that over the past few hours. So it could be that's where the impact will be felt coming out of the summit.
paragraphs.cn: 不仅是对乌克兰的强力支持，可能还有来自盟友的资金支持，而且美国强调 --  -- 美国愿意再次与俄罗斯进行谈判。普京和特朗普在过去几小时内已经讨论过这一点。所以，这可能是峰会带来的影响所在。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Moscow saying that Putin held a 90-minute phone call with the President over the past couple of days here as we're thinking through what you just mentioned and where the Russia-Ukraine war could actually see some type of material movement. How would this NATO summit set the stage for those talks and a ceasefire but even more so an end to that war?
paragraphs.cn: 莫斯科表示，普京在过去几天与总统进行了90分钟的电话通话，我们正在思考你刚才提到的内容，以及俄乌战争可能取得实质性进展的地方。这次北约峰会如何为这些谈判和停火，甚至结束战争奠定基础？

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Right. And I think if we can get a ceasefire, that would be a great step forward. But you're absolutely right. I think coming out of this summit, if Putin sees that the alliance is unified, that there's not splits that he can exploit, if Putin can see that the alliance is unified, the assistance is going to continue and the financial assistance will be also discussed and some things announced, I hope, during the summit about further financial assistance to Ukraine.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。我认为如果我们能实现停火，那将是一个巨大的进步。但你说得完全正确。我认为在这次峰会之后，如果普京看到联盟是团结的，没有他可以利用的分歧，如果普京看到联盟是团结的，援助将继续，财政援助也将被讨论，我希望在峰会期间会宣布一些关于进一步援助乌克兰的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: Those signals that Putin will get is that Europe isn't going anywhere in terms of their support for Ukraine and the United States has stepped into the void. If we say something at the summit, which I think we will, that the U.S. is willing to step back into the void in terms of negotiations and try to start things back up again.
paragraphs.cn: 普京将得到的信号是，欧洲对乌克兰的支持不会改变，而美国已经填补了空白。如果我们在峰会上说些什么 --  -- 我认为我们会说 --  -- 美国愿意重新回到谈判的空白中，并试图重新启动进程。

paragraphs.speaker: Townsend
paragraphs.text: So, this summit can set the stage of willingness on the West, number one, to support Ukraine, and number two, to step in and try to broker some type of ceasefire that could lead to a long-standing peace.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这次峰会可以奠定西方的意愿：第一，支持乌克兰；第二，介入并试图促成某种停火，从而带来持久的和平。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Jim Townsend, really valuable insight. Thank you so much for joining us.
paragraphs.cn: 吉姆·汤森德，非常有价值的见解。非常感谢你加入我们。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Well, a new presidential library now open to the public in North Dakota. The 96,000-square-foot Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library highlights the 26th president's legacy and the deep ties to the American West. It cost about $450 million to build, and here's what some of the first visitors had to say.
paragraphs.cn: 一座新的总统图书馆现已向北达科他州的公众开放。这座96000平方英尺的西奥多·罗斯福总统图书馆突出了第26任总统的遗产以及与美国西部的深厚联系。建造费用约为4.5亿美元，以下是首批参观者的一些评价。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: History is one of my favorite things to study and to be a part of it is a huge honor.
paragraphs.cn: Sejarah adalah salah satu hal yang paling saya sukai untuk dipelajari, dan bisa menjadi bagian di dalamnya adalah suatu kehormatan besar.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: We wanted him to get to experience something he's heard about and wrote about, but now he gets to be a living part of it.
paragraphs.cn: Kami berharap dia bisa merasakan langsung hal-hal yang pernah dia dengar dan tulis, dan sekarang dia menjadi bagian dari itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The museum takes visitors through Roosevelt's life from his childhood in New York to his time out West. Many of the exhibits are interactive with designers using technology to keep people engaged and exploring. Officials say that the library could draw as many as 200,000 visitors each year. Well, for Charlie Kirk's family, this week marks another pivotal moment in court. His accused killer faces a hearing that could pave the way for a death penalty trial. We'll break down what is at stake.
paragraphs.cn: Museum ini membawa pengunjung menelusuri kehidupan Roosevelt dari masa kecilnya di New York hingga masa-masa di Barat. Banyak pameran yang interaktif, dan para desainer menggunakan teknologi untuk membuat pengunjung tetap terlibat dan mengeksplorasi. Pejabat mengatakan perpustakaan ini mungkin menarik hingga 200.000 pengunjung per tahun. Bagi keluarga Charlie Kirk, minggu ini ada momen penting lainnya di pengadilan. Tersangka pembunuhnya akan menghadapi sidang yang bisa membuka jalan menuju persidangan hukuman mati. Kami akan menganalisis apa yang dipertaruhkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: And if you're heading out, remember that you can stream our show from anywhere in the U.S. right from the CNN app. You can also go to cnn.com/watch.
paragraphs.cn: Jika Anda akan keluar rumah, ingatlah bahwa Anda dapat menonton acara kami di mana saja di Amerika melalui aplikasi CNN. Anda juga dapat mengunjungi cnn.com/watch.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: A critical preliminary hearing starts this week for the accused killer of conservative activist Charlie Kirk. A judge will decide if there is probable cause to support a slew of charges against 23-year-old Tyler Robinson. The hearing is expected to take several days. Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, and his parents are expected to be there. Prosecutors have already said that they plan to seek the death penalty against Robinson if he's convicted. And joining me now, we've got retired judge Ashley Willcott.
paragraphs.cn: Minggu ini, tersangka pembunuh aktivis konservatif Charlie Kirk akan memulai sidang pendahuluan yang kritis. Hakim akan memutuskan apakah ada alasan yang cukup untuk mendukung serangkaian tuduhan terhadap Tyler Robinson, 23 tahun. Sidang diperkirakan akan berlangsung beberapa hari. Janda Kirk, Erika Kirk, dan orang tuanya diperkirakan akan hadir. Jaksa telah menyatakan bahwa jika Robinson dihukum, mereka berencana untuk menuntut hukuman mati. Bergabung dengan kami sekarang adalah pensiunan hakim Ashley Wilcott.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Good to have you here with us on this matter.
paragraphs.cn: Senang Anda bisa bersama kami untuk membahas masalah ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: I mean, we're all going to be watching to see how this plays out, but let's just talk about the expected evidence here. The prosecution's key witness, Robinson's former roommate.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, kita semua akan mengikuti perkembangan, tapi mari kita bicarakan bukti yang diharapkan. Saksi kunci penuntut, mantan teman sekamar Robinson.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: What is the information that they will be looking to deploy, assess and really bring to the court as they go through this case?
paragraphs.cn: Dalam menangani kasus ini, informasi apa yang akan mereka coba gunakan, evaluasi, dan benar-benar berikan ke pengadilan?

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Great question. So, remember, preliminary hearing, it's a low standard probable cause. So, the prosecution isn't necessarily going to present all of its evidence, but rather strategically the evidence that has to present for the judge to say there's enough for this to go forward to trial.
paragraphs.cn: Pertanyaan bagus. Ingat, standar untuk sidang pendahuluan adalah 'alasan yang masuk akal' yang lebih rendah. Jadi, penuntut tidak harus menunjukkan semua bukti, tetapi secara strategis menunjukkan bukti yang cukup untuk membuat hakim percaya bahwa kasus ini bisa dilanjutkan ke persidangan.

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Knowing that, I expect when you speak of the former roommate, for instance, we know that there were texts allegedly between the defendant and the roommate about don't talk, don't tell them anything, look under the computer keyboard. There's a note. And that note said, if I have the opportunity, I'm going to take out Charlie Kirk. So, a lot of incriminating evidence from that roommate.
paragraphs.cn: Mengetahui hal itu, saya perkirakan ketika Anda menyebut mantan teman sekamar, misalnya, kita tahu ada dugaan SMS antara terdakwa dan teman sekamarnya yang berisi 'Jangan bicara, jangan beri tahu mereka apa pun, lihat di bawah keyboard komputer. Ada catatan.' Catatan itu bertuliskan: 'Jika ada kesempatan, saya akan menghabisi Charlie Kirk.' Jadi, teman sekamar itu memberikan banyak bukti yang memberatkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: With all of the charges that we're looking at, and we're talking about charges like aggravated murder, felony use of a firearm, obstruction of justice. What -- and put yourself in the judge's shoes here. Do we think that the judge will decide that there's probable cause to support all of them or handpicked some of them? How would you be going through?
paragraphs.cn: Mengingat semua tuduhan yang kita hadapi, seperti pembunuhan berat, penggunaan senjata api dengan kejahatan berat, menghalangi keadilan, dll. Jika Anda berada di posisi hakim, menurut Anda apakah hakim akan memutuskan bahwa ada alasan yang cukup untuk semua tuduhan, atau hanya memilih beberapa? Bagaimana Anda akan menanganinya?

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: That's a great question. If it were me, I would have those charges in front of me listed as well as the definitions. As I listen to the prosecution's evidence, if it were me, I'm going to be checking off and writing, oh, this supports this charge. I'm not sure the obstruction of justice, both charges are supported by the evidence. And then I would go through in my ruling and say this one was supported by this each charge. Now, the judge has discretion.
paragraphs.cn: Pertanyaan bagus. Jika saya, saya akan meletakkan tuduhan-tuduhan itu dan definisinya di depan saya. Saat mendengarkan bukti dari jaksa, jika saya, saya akan mencoret-coret sambil memeriksa, seperti 'Oh, ini mendukung tuduhan ini', 'Saya tidak yakin apakah kedua tuduhan menghalangi keadilan didukung oleh bukti'. Kemudian saya akan menjelaskan satu per satu dalam putusan apakah setiap tuduhan didukung oleh bukti. Hakim memiliki kebijaksanaan.

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: If the judge does not see the evidence there to either a dismiss any of those charges, all of those charges, or can say you may have evidence for this, but not this particular charge. And then the prosecution can change the charges.
paragraphs.cn: Jika hakim menganggap bukti tidak cukup untuk menolak salah satu atau semua tuduhan, atau bisa dikatakan 'Anda punya bukti untuk tuduhan ini, tapi tidak untuk yang itu', maka jaksa dapat mengubah tuduhan.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The death penalty here.
paragraphs.cn: Di sini ada hukuman mati.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: And so, this is what prosecutors have already said that they are going to be seeking. What in the judge's shoes then are they going to be looking forward to ultimately decide whether or not that remains on the table?
paragraphs.cn: Jaksa telah menyatakan akan menuntut hukuman mati. Jadi dari sudut pandang hakim, bagaimana pada akhirnya mereka akan memutuskan apakah akan mempertahankan opsi hukuman mati?

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Well, you know, what's interesting, that's not going to be decided at the preliminary hearing. If the charge of aggravated murder remains after the probable cause hearing, then the state from arraignment has 60 days to file the formal notice to pursue the death penalty. And so, it doesn't make a difference for the preliminary hearing, but aggravated murder is the charge that the judge has to find is supported by enough evidence to go forward in order for the state to then pursue the death penalty.
paragraphs.cn: Menariknya, ini tidak akan diputuskan dalam sidang pendahuluan. Jika tuduhan pembunuhan berat tetap ada setelah sidang kemungkinan penyebab, maka negara bagian memiliki waktu 60 hari setelah dakwaan untuk mengajukan pemberitahuan resmi untuk menuntut hukuman mati. Jadi, ini tidak berpengaruh pada sidang pendahuluan, tetapi hakim harus menetapkan bahwa tuduhan pembunuhan berat didukung oleh bukti yang cukup agar negara bagian dapat melanjutkan tuntutan hukuman mati.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: This is a very high-profile case.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah kasus yang sangat diperhatikan.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: When you think about the number of eyeballs that are going to be on this and the number of focus that this court is going to have on it, both state, federally, what does that imply for just how this could and should proceed at the end of the day?
paragraphs.cn: Mengingat kasus ini akan mendapat banyak perhatian, baik di tingkat negara bagian maupun federal, apa artinya ini bagi bagaimana kasus ini akhirnya harus berjalan?

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Oh, boy, this judge will have to dot every I, cross every T because we know the judge has already said preliminary hearing expected to last the week. There are going to be cameras so that the public can have an unfettered view of what happens inside that courtroom. Transparency. But the court also has to balance that to make sure the defendant's constitutional rights are protected.
paragraphs.cn: Ya ampun, hakim ini harus benar-benar sempurna, karena hakim telah mengatakan sidang pendahuluan diperkirakan akan berlangsung seminggu. Kamera akan dipasang di ruang sidang, memungkinkan publik untuk melihat proses persidangan tanpa hambatan. Ini adalah transparansi. Tapi pengadilan juga harus menyeimbangkan ini, memastikan hak konstitusional terdakwa dilindungi.

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: So, for instance, the judge has said, journalists that come in, you cannot bring your phone, you cannot bring your laptop, you're going to have to rely on what's videoed. So, the judge throughout this is going to have to be very cautious to make certain that the public interest, court of public opinion, is taken into account but doesn't influence or affect negatively all of the defendant's constitutional rights. It's a balancing act.
paragraphs.cn: Misalnya, hakim telah menetapkan bahwa wartawan yang masuk tidak boleh membawa ponsel dan laptop, hanya boleh mengandalkan rekaman video. Jadi, hakim harus sangat berhati-hati selama proses, memastikan kepentingan publik dan pengadilan opini publik dipertimbangkan, tetapi tidak mempengaruhi atau merusak hak konstitusional terdakwa. Ini adalah keseimbangan.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: A fair and speedy trial.
paragraphs.cn: Pengadilan yang adil dan cepat.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: How much more difficult does that become?
paragraphs.cn: Seberapa sulitkah itu nantinya?

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Yes, very difficult. However, it's the defense's strategy to determine are they going to demand that, in which case it goes very quickly and the prosecution can sometimes feel like it's scurrying around to get everything done timely or sometimes the defense says, no, we want it to go longer before we actually go to trial. We want it to become less than the public opinion. That's all a strategy decision by the defense.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, sangat sulit. Namun, strategi pembela adalah memutuskan apakah mereka akan meminta itu, jika demikian, semuanya akan berjalan cepat, jaksa kadang merasa kewalahan untuk menyelesaikan semua tepat waktu; atau pembela kadang berkata, tidak, kami ingin menunda lebih lama sebelum persidangan sebenarnya. Kami ingin ini menjadi kurang dipengaruhi opini publik. Ini semua adalah keputusan strategis pembela.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Interesting. Ashley Willcott, thank you so much.
paragraphs.cn: Menarik. Ashley Wilcott, terima kasih banyak.

paragraphs.speaker: Willcott
paragraphs.text: Thank you. Good to see you.
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih. Senang bertemu denganmu.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Really valuable insight. Well, a quiet English town is thrust into the global spotlight when a mysterious poisoning reveals a deadly international conspiracy. Here's a sneak peek from our latest CNN film.
paragraphs.cn: Wawasan yang sangat berharga. Sebuah kota kecil yang tenang di Inggris mendadak menjadi sorotan global setelah insiden peracunan misterius mengungkap konspirasi internasional yang mematikan. Berikut adalah cuplikan film CNN terbaru kami.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Salisbury is a small city. What happened doesn't sound like it's something that happens in your town.
paragraphs.cn: Salisbury adalah kota kecil. Apa yang terjadi terdengar seperti bukan hal yang biasa terjadi di kotamu.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Two people were having a medical episode.
paragraphs.cn: Ada dua orang yang mengalami kondisi medis.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Immediately, this didn't look right. They had very unusual symptoms. Somebody said, you're not going to believe what I've just found out. Something has poisoned these people.
paragraphs.cn: Seketika, ini tampak tidak benar. Mereka memiliki gejala yang sangat tidak biasa. Seseorang berkata, 'Kamu tidak akan percaya apa yang baru saja kutemukan.' Seseorang meracuni orang-orang ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: It turns out that he was a Russian spy.
paragraphs.cn: Ternyata dia adalah seorang mata-mata Rusia.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: We were told that an agent had been used.
paragraphs.cn: Kami diberitahu bahwa suatu agen digunakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: How did it get into the country and where was it now?
paragraphs.cn: Bagaimana ia masuk ke negara ini? Dan sekarang di mana?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Salisbury has taken a serious turn for the worst. Now it's murder.
paragraphs.cn: Insiden Salisbury memburuk secara serius, dan sekarang telah meningkat menjadi kasus pembunuhan.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: There was enough poison in that bottle to kill 10,000 people. There is no playbook for this.
paragraphs.cn: Racun dalam botol itu cukup untuk membunuh sepuluh ribu orang. Tidak ada preseden untuk ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Russia had been responsible for this.
paragraphs.cn: Rusia bertanggung jawab atas hal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: There were people who thought we should treat this attack as an act of war.
paragraphs.cn: Beberapa orang berpikir kita harus menganggap serangan ini sebagai tindakan perang.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: The poisoning was just the beginning. This is bigger than we thought.
paragraphs.cn: Peracunan hanyalah awal, ini jauh lebih serius dari yang kita kira.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: There comes a point where you have to tell the world what you found.
paragraphs.cn: Pada suatu titik, kamu harus memberi tahu dunia apa yang kamu temukan.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: The ugliness of the world was suddenly on our doorstep.
paragraphs.cn: Keburukan dunia tiba-tiba datang ke depan pintu kami.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: "The Salisbury Poisonings: A Spy Next Door," premieres July 12th on CNN and next day on the CNN app.
paragraphs.cn: 'The Salisbury Poisonings: Spy Among Friends' tayang perdana di CNN pada 12 Juli, dan dapat ditonton di aplikasi CNN keesokan harinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Let's get a check of your top stories. Extreme heat and severe storms delayed, but did not stop the 4th of July celebrations around the country. In the nation's capital, crowds packed the National Mall to watch what was billed as the largest fireworks display in history. President Trump spoke at the event touting American strength and warning against the threat of communism. President Trump heads to a major NATO summit in Turkey this week. Ongoing wars between Russia and Ukraine and the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: Mari kita lihat berita utama. Meskipun gelombang panas ekstrem dan badai hebat menyebabkan penundaan, itu tidak menghentikan perayaan Hari Kemerdekaan di seluruh negeri. Di ibu kota, kerumunan memenuhi National Mall untuk menyaksikan pertunjukan kembang api yang diklaim sebagai yang terbesar dalam sejarah. Presiden Trump berpidato dalam acara tersebut, mempromosikan kekuatan Amerika dan memperingatkan ancaman komunis. Presiden Trump minggu ini akan pergi ke Turki untuk menghadiri KTT NATO yang penting. Perang antara Rusia dan Ukraina serta Amerika masih berlangsung.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: and Iran likely to be discussed. Trump has a well-documented dislike for NATO, but the organization still reached out to wish the U.S. a happy birthday this weekend on social media. And Pope Leo is urging Americans to welcome immigrants with, quote, "compassion and generosity." He delivered the message during a July 4th visit to the Italian island of Lampedusa, a major arrival point for migrants crossing the Mediterranean.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah Iran kemungkinan akan dibahas. Ketidaksukaan Trump terhadap NATO sudah tercatat, tetapi organisasi tersebut tetap mengirimkan ucapan ulang tahun kepada Amerika melalui media sosial akhir pekan lalu. Paus Leo mendesak orang Amerika untuk menyambut imigran dengan 'belas kasih dan kemurahan hati.' Dia menyampaikan pesan ini saat mengunjungi pulau Lampedusa, Italia, pada 4 Juli, yang merupakan titik pendaratan utama bagi imigran yang melintasi Laut Mediterania.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: And this morning, we are getting a first glimpse inside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. Ana Demmer, who is engaged to Kansas City Chiefs player Creed Humphrey, posted a photo on Instagram of a handkerchief that appears to have been given to guests Friday night. The handkerchief is embroidered with TNT, the date, and New York City, along with a lyric from "Blank Space." So, it's going to be forever. And Parisians are now able to get a break from sweltering heat by taking a dip in the Seine River.
paragraphs.cn: Pagi ini, kami melihat sekilas interior pernikahan Taylor Swift dan Travis Kelce. Anna Demer, yang bertunangan dengan pemain Kansas City Chiefs, Creed Humphrey, mengunggah foto sapu tangan di Instagram yang tampaknya diberikan kepada tamu pada Jumat malam. Sapu tangan itu dibordir dengan TNT, tanggal, dan New York City, serta lirik dari 'Blank Space.' Jadi, ini akan abadi. Warga Paris kini bisa berenang di Sungai Seine untuk melepaskan diri dari panas yang menyengat.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: It was open to the public on Saturday and for a second year in a row, even though swimming was off limits for decades because of pollution. Melissa Bell explains how the river was transformed. (BEGIN VIDEOTAPE) MELISSA BELL, CNN SENIOR INTERNATIONAL CORRESPONDENT (voice-over): Forget climbing the Eiffel Tower. The cool new thing to do in Paris is to swim beneath it.
paragraphs.cn: Meskipun berenang telah dilarang selama beberapa dekade karena polusi, Sungai Seine dibuka untuk umum pada hari Sabtu, dan ini adalah tahun kedua berturut-turut. Melissa Bell menjelaskan bagaimana sungai itu diubah.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: It's very quiet. Yes, very nice, and you can see some very little fish in the water, yes.
paragraphs.cn: Di sini sangat tenang. Ya, sangat bagus, kamu bisa melihat ikan-ikan kecil di air, ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: We swim in Copenhagen, where we come from as well. So, we don't really mind, actually. BELL (voice-over): Others are still a little hesitant.
paragraphs.cn: Kami berenang di Kopenhagen, itu juga kota asal kami. Jadi, kami sebenarnya tidak keberatan. Bell (voiceover): Yang lain masih ragu-ragu.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I have not, like, entered the water, but I think the idea of, like, lounging by the Seine is really cool. BELL (voice-over): It's hard to imagine that the Seine River was once a toxic mess, home to half of Paris' untreated wastewater. In fact, for a hundred years, swimming in it was strictly prohibited. Now, for the first time, for the second year in a row, public swimming areas will be open till the end of August.
paragraphs.cn: Saya belum masuk air, tapi saya pikir ide nongkrong di tepi Sungai Seine sangat keren. Bell (voiceover): Sulit membayangkan Sungai Seine dulunya adalah kekacauan beracun, di mana setengah dari air limbah Paris yang tidak diolah dibuang ke dalamnya. Faktanya, selama seratus tahun, berenang di sungai sangat dilarang. Sekarang, untuk tahun kedua berturut-turut, area renang umum dibuka untuk pertama kalinya hingga akhir Agustus.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: First, I would like to guarantee them that the quality of the water is very good. Secondly, it's an amazing occasion to discover Paris in a different way. BELL (voice-over): A swimmable Seine was one of the pledges of the 2024 Paris Olympics. 1.4 billion euros were spent getting the river clean enough for the athletes to compete in it, but the idea was always to give ordinary Parisians their river back, too.
paragraphs.cn: Pertama, saya ingin meyakinkan mereka bahwa kualitas air sangat baik. Kedua, ini adalah kesempatan luar biasa untuk menemukan Paris dengan cara yang berbeda. Bell (voiceover): Sungai Seine yang bisa berenang adalah salah satu janji Olimpiade Paris 2024. 1,4 miliar euro dihabiskan untuk membuat sungai cukup bersih bagi atlet untuk bertanding, tetapi idenya selalu juga untuk mengembalikan sungai kepada warga Paris biasa.

paragraphs.speaker: Bell
paragraphs.text: A few years ago, the idea of swimming in the Seine River would have seemed crazy, but then again, this is a city in which it is often said that the only sane thing is the river.
paragraphs.cn: Beberapa tahun lalu, ide berenang di Sungai Seine terdengar gila, tapi lagi-lagi, ini adalah kota yang sering dikatakan satu-satunya hal yang masuk akal adalah sungainya.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. Melissa Bell, thank you. Well, this morning, heat advisories remain in effect across several parts of the U.S., but some relief may finally be on the way. CNN Meteorologist Allison Chinchar with me now. Allison, how much longer does this heat stick around and who gets relief first?
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Melissa Bell, terima kasih. Pagi ini, banyak wilayah di AS masih dalam peringatan panas, tapi beberapa keringanan mungkin akhirnya tiba. Ahli meteorologi CNN Allison Chinchar bersama saya sekarang. Allison, berapa lama panas ini akan bertahan? Siapa yang akan mendapat keringanan pertama?

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Well, some will start to get relief as early as today. Others will have to wait another day or so, but it is on the way. One thing we're also keeping an eye on are all of these storms across the area. You can see we've got some in the southeast, the Midwest, even across portions of the northeast. We've had some warnings off and on throughout the morning so far across areas of the southeast.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, beberapa orang akan mulai mendapat keringanan paling awal hari ini. Yang lain mungkin harus menunggu satu hari lagi, tapi keringanan akan segera tiba. Kami juga memperhatikan semua badai di wilayah ini. Anda bisa melihat badai di Tenggara, Midwest, bahkan sebagian Timur Laut. Sejauh ini, ada peringatan yang terputus-putus di wilayah Tenggara sepanjang pagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Right now, we are warning free, but you can still see a lot of lightning with those storms in Louisiana and portions of Mississippi. Lots of lightning here with this system in Michigan. It's making its way towards Detroit and Toledo. That will continue to slide off to the east. The greatest threat for today in terms of severe thunderstorms exists essentially from the Texas panhandle all the way up through New York.
paragraphs.cn: Saat ini, kami dalam status tanpa peringatan, tapi Anda masih bisa melihat banyak petir di badai di sebagian Louisiana dan Mississippi. Sistem cuaca di Michigan ini juga membawa banyak petir, bergerak menuju Detroit dan Toledo, dan akan terus bergerak ke timur. Hari ini, ancaman terbesar badai petir kuat membentang dari Texas Panhandle hingga New York.

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: That's where we could be looking at damaging winds and even some small hail mixed in with some of those storms. Not to mention we have the ones this morning, but it really starts to ramp up as we go through the day, especially across Illinois, Missouri, and then that line of storms that is expected to form stretching all the way back into Texas. These will continue into the evening and overnight hours for many of these areas as well. Now, we talked about relief from the heat.
paragraphs.cn: Wilayah-wilayah ini mungkin mengalami angin merusak, bahkan hujan es kecil di beberapa badai. Belum lagi kami sudah mengalami beberapa badai pagi ini, tapi situasi akan benar-benar meningkat seiring waktu, terutama di Illinois, Missouri, dan kemudian diperkirakan akan terbentuk garis badai yang membentang hingga Texas. Badai ini akan berlanjut hingga malam dan malam hari, mempengaruhi banyak wilayah. Sekarang, mari kita bicara tentang keringanan panas.

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Starting today in a place like Chicago, only 75 today. Their normal is 84, but they'll be right back up to it by the middle of the week. Washington, D.C., topping at 95 today. So, still one more day of the heat, but then we start to see those temperatures dipping back down and actually to even below average by the time we get to the middle of the week. Take a look at this, Tuesday and Wednesday, temperature is about 5 degrees below average, but then we get back up to the 90s by Friday.
paragraphs.cn: Mulai hari ini, tempat-tempat seperti Chicago hanya 75 derajat Fahrenheit hari ini. Suhu normal mereka 84 derajat Fahrenheit, tapi akan kembali normal pada pertengahan minggu. Washington DC hari ini maksimum 95 derajat Fahrenheit. Jadi, panas akan berlangsung satu hari lagi, tapi kemudian kita akan melihat suhu turun, bahkan di bawah rata-rata pada pertengahan minggu. Lihat ini, Selasa dan Rabu, suhu sekitar 5 derajat Fahrenheit di bawah rata-rata, tapi akan kembali ke 90-an pada hari Jumat.

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: And so, you're going to see kind of that up-and-down pattern here too. Out to the west, we also have some heat warnings. That, guys, is in effect starting Tuesday as more of the heat returns to the west.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Anda akan melihat pola naik-turun ini. Di Barat, kami juga memiliki beberapa peringatan panas. Teman-teman, ini mulai berlaku pada hari Selasa, karena Barat akan kembali mengalami lebih banyak panas.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. Allison Chinchar, thank you so much. Going to be watching that close. A major upset at Wimbledon. The reigning women's champ eliminated. Coy Wire is here with me now.
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Allison Chinchar, terima kasih banyak. Kami akan terus memantau. Kejutan besar di Kejuaraan Wimbledon. Juara bertahan tunggal putri tersingkir. Coy Wire bersama saya sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Coy Wire
paragraphs.text: Yes, Iga Swiatek, down. And Alexandra Eala, she says that she doesn't think there are any grass courts in the Philippines where she grew up. She said she grew up playing on a court where a basketball court was painted on top of a tennis court and there were actually basketball hoops. So, she couldn't really back up or she'd hit the basketball post. Huge upset taking down Iga Swiatek, the reigning champ. This buzzsaw named Alex Eala. She's 21 years old from the Philippines. She won in straight sets.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, Iga Swiatek tersingkir. Dan Alexandra Eala mengatakan dia pikir Filipina tempat dia dibesarkan tidak memiliki lapangan rumput. Dia mengatakan dia bermain di lapangan yang dilukis lapangan basket di atas lapangan tenis, di mana sebenarnya ada tiang basket. Jadi dia tidak bisa mundur terlalu jauh atau akan menabrak tiang basket. Dia mengalahkan juara bertahan Iga Swiatek. Pemain bernama Alex Eala ini, 21 tahun, dari Filipina. Dia menang straight set.

paragraphs.speaker: Coy Wire
paragraphs.text: First Filipino player ever to reach the fourth round of a Grand Slam singles tournament. Listen to the emotion that came pouring out of her after this win.
paragraphs.cn: Dia adalah pemain Filipina pertama yang mencapai babak keempat turnamen Grand Slam tunggal. Dengarkan emosi yang meluap setelah kemenangannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Alexandra Eala
paragraphs.text: For someone who grew up in the Philippines, and I went to train with my brother and my grandfather every day after school with my ruffled socks and my light-up shoes and chubby cheeks. So, to her, this is everything. But obviously, because I'm emotional does not mean I'm satisfied. So, yes, OK, next round. Let's go.
paragraphs.cn: Bagi seseorang yang dibesarkan di Filipina, saya berlatih setiap hari sepulang sekolah dengan kaus kaki kusut, sepatu berkilau, pipi tembem, bersama saudara laki-laki dan kakek saya. Jadi, baginya, ini adalah segalanya. Tapi jelas, saya emosional bukan berarti saya puas. Jadi, ya, oke, babak berikutnya. Ayo.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: So good. All right. not so good for tennis fans. Serena Williams says she's heartbroken to withdraw from the doubles tournament with her sister Venus after she hurt her knee in a singles match last week. She even posted photos of all the fluids she had removed from her knees, and coming back to compete again has been a gift. Telling fans to stay tuned, though, to a city near you. All right.
paragraphs.cn: Luar biasa. Baik. Tidak terlalu baik bagi penggemar tenis. Serena Williams mengatakan dia patah hati karena harus mundur dari ganda bersama saudara perempuannya Venus karena cedera lutut di pertandingan tunggal. Dia bahkan memposting foto cairan yang dikeluarkan dari lututnya dan mengatakan bisa bermain lagi adalah anugerah. Namun, dia menyuruh penggemar untuk memperhatikan kota-kota yang akan mereka kunjungi. Baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: France marching on, Kylian Mbappe burying this penalty in the 70th minute to beat Paraguay 1-nil and book a spot in the quarterfinals. Goal number seven, tying him with Messi for the most in the tourney so far. Mbappe collecting goals like Pokemon. France will face Morocco next. All right. Avert your eyes if you are currently eating or get queasy easily. This is how a man gains about 24 pounds in just 10 minutes.
paragraphs.cn: Prancis melanjutkan, Kylian Mbappe mencetak penalti pada menit ke-70 untuk menang 1-0 atas Paraguay, mengamankan tempat di perempat final. Itu gol ketujuhnya, menyamai Messi sebagai pencetak gol terbanyak turnamen. Mbappe mengumpulkan gol seperti mengumpulkan Pokemon. Prancis akan menghadapi Maroko selanjutnya. Baik, jika Anda sedang makan atau mudah mual, alihkan pandangan. Inilah bagaimana seseorang bisa menambah berat sekitar 24 pon dalam 10 menit.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: Joey Chestnut winning his 18th mustard belt, devouring 66 hot dogs, buns, and all that water in 10 minutes at the annual Nathan's Famous International Hot Dog Eating Contest. That's won every 9.1 seconds. And for some perspective, though we probably don't want it, 66 hot dogs stacked end-to-end would stretch about 40 feet. That's about three to four stories high. So, I want to pose a question to my friends here.
paragraphs.cn: Joey Chestnut memenangkan sabuk mustard ke-18 di Kontes Hot Dog Internasional Nathan ke-108, memakan 66 hot dog, roti, dan semuanya dalam 10 menit. Rata-rata satu hot dog setiap 9,1 detik. Sebagai perbandingan, meskipun kita mungkin tidak ingin tahu, 66 hot dog yang dijejerkan panjangnya sekitar 40 kaki, setinggi tiga atau empat lantai. Jadi, saya ingin mengajukan pertanyaan kepada teman-teman di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: If you had to eat one food in a contest, which one would you eat just because you love it? I'll go first. I'm going chicken wings, especially that lemon pepper variety.
paragraphs.cn: Jika Anda harus makan satu jenis makanan dalam kontes, apa yang akan Anda pilih, hanya karena Anda menyukainya? Saya duluan. Saya pilih sayap ayam, terutama yang lada lemon.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: I'm going snow crab legs.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pilih kaki kepiting salju.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: I thought you said tater tots.
paragraphs.cn: Saya kira Anda bilang bola kentang.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Yes, I was thinking about it a little bit more, though. Anything worth doing is worth doing well. Allison?
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya pikir lagi. Apa pun yang layak dilakukan, layak dilakukan dengan baik. Allison?

paragraphs.speaker: Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Reese's peanut butter cups.
paragraphs.cn: Cangkir selai kacang Reese.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: This girl keeps them stacked in a drawer at work.
paragraphs.cn: Gadis ini menimbun banyak di laci mejanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Breakfast of champions.
paragraphs.cn: Sarapan juara.

paragraphs.speaker: Wire
paragraphs.text: Hey, no shame. Like, I partake of this.
paragraphs.cn: Hei, tidak memalukan. Saya juga makan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: I've got to get invited to that drawer and find out where that's at. Thank you guys so much. Really fun. Coy Wire, appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: Saya harus diundang untuk melihat di mana laci itu. Terima kasih banyak, sangat menyenangkan. Coy Wire, terima kasih.

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Allison, good to see you. Thank you so much for joining us on CNN This Morning Weekend. Inside Politics Sunday with Manu Raju is up next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Allison, senang bertemu Anda. Terima kasih banyak telah bergabung di CNN This Morning Weekend. Selanjutnya adalah Inside Politics Sunday dengan Manu Raju.
